name: Southwestern Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Red bell pepper, core seed
2 cups Cooked white beans, drained and rinsed
1 cup Tomatoes, peeled and seeded, chopped
recipeInstructions:
Note on Avocado: Peel, pit, chop, and toss with a little lime juice.
Heat 1 tablespoon of the oil over medium heat in a large heavy pot. Add the onion, garlic, cumin, oregano, jalapeno, and bell pepper and cook, stirring until the onion is transparent, about 6 to 8 minutes. Add the broth and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes. Add the corn, lime juice, beans, tomatoes, avocado, and cilantro, then remove from the heat. Season with salt and pepper.
While the soup is simmering, heat the remaining oil in a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Then fry the tortilla strips until crisp, about 3 to 5 minutes. Drain on towels. Serve several strips of the crispy tortillas in each bowl, then pour in the soup.
